result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  collect      Monitor logs and metrics in a .NET application send the results to a chosen destination.
  config       Configuration related commands for dotnet-monitor.
  generatekey  Generate api key and hash for authentication.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  collect      Monitor logs and metrics in a .NET application send the results to a chosen destination.
  config       Configuration related commands for dotnet-monitor.
  generatekey  Generate api key and hash for authentication.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Monitor logs and metrics in a .NET application send the results to a chosen destination.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor collect [options]

Options:
  -u, --urls <urls>                                  Bindings for the REST api. [default: https://localhost:52323]
  --metricUrls <metricUrls>                          Bindings for metrics api. [default: http://localhost:52325]
  -m, --metrics <metrics>                            Enable publishing of metrics
  --diagnostic-port <diagnosticPort>                 The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.
  --no-auth <noAuth>                                 Turn off authentication.
  --temp-apikey <tempApiKey>                         Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process.
  --no-http-egress <noHttpEgress>                    Turn off HTTP response egress
  --configuration-file-path <configurationFilePath>  The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.
  --exit-on-stdin-disconnect                         If specified, dotnet-monitor will monitor `stdin`, and if it closes, will exit. This is useful when dotnet-monitor is started remotely without a pseudo-terminal, and the caller wishes for it to exit when the connection is dropped (example: `docker exec -i ...`).
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Monitor logs and metrics in a .NET application send the results to a chosen destination.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor collect [options]

Options:
  -u, --urls <urls>                                  Bindings for the REST api. [default: https://localhost:52323]
  --metricUrls <metricUrls>                          Bindings for metrics api. [default: http://localhost:52325]
  -m, --metrics <metrics>                            Enable publishing of metrics
  --diagnostic-port <diagnosticPort>                 The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.
  --no-auth <noAuth>                                 Turn off authentication.
  --temp-apikey <tempApiKey>                         Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process.
  --no-http-egress <noHttpEgress>                    Turn off HTTP response egress
  --configuration-file-path <configurationFilePath>  The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.
  --exit-on-stdin-disconnect                         If specified, dotnet-monitor will monitor `stdin`, and if it closes, will exit. This is useful when dotnet-monitor is started remotely without a pseudo-terminal, and the caller wishes for it to exit when the connection is dropped (example: `docker exec -i ...`).
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Configuration related commands for dotnet-monitor.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor config [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  show  Shows configuration, as if dotnet-monitor collect was executed with these parameters.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Configuration related commands for dotnet-monitor.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor config [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  show  Shows configuration, as if dotnet-monitor collect was executed with these parameters.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Shows configuration, as if dotnet-monitor collect was executed with these parameters.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor config show [options]

Options:
  -u, --urls <urls>                                  Bindings for the REST api. [default: https://localhost:52323]
  --metricUrls <metricUrls>                          Bindings for metrics api. [default: http://localhost:52325]
  -m, --metrics <metrics>                            Enable publishing of metrics
  --diagnostic-port <diagnosticPort>                 The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.
  --no-auth <noAuth>                                 Turn off authentication.
  --temp-apikey <tempApiKey>                         Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process.
  --no-http-egress <noHttpEgress>                    Turn off HTTP response egress
  --configuration-file-path <configurationFilePath>  The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.
  --level <level>                                    Configuration level. Unredacted configuration can show sensitive information. [default: Redacted]
  --show-sources <showSources>                       Show loaded configuration providers from high to low priority
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Shows configuration, as if dotnet-monitor collect was executed with these parameters.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor config show [options]

Options:
  -u, --urls <urls>                                  Bindings for the REST api. [default: https://localhost:52323]
  --metricUrls <metricUrls>                          Bindings for metrics api. [default: http://localhost:52325]
  -m, --metrics <metrics>                            Enable publishing of metrics
  --diagnostic-port <diagnosticPort>                 The fully qualified path and filename of the diagnostic port to which runtime instances can connect.
  --no-auth <noAuth>                                 Turn off authentication.
  --temp-apikey <tempApiKey>                         Generates a new MonitorApiKey for each launch of the process.
  --no-http-egress <noHttpEgress>                    Turn off HTTP response egress
  --configuration-file-path <configurationFilePath>  The fully qualified path and filename of the json configuration file you'd like to add to the list of configuration sources.
  --level <level>                                    Configuration level. Unredacted configuration can show sensitive information. [default: Redacted]
  --show-sources <showSources>                       Show loaded configuration providers from high to low priority
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate api key and hash for authentication.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor generatekey [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <output>          The output format of the API Key configuration. [default: Json]
  -e, --expiration <expiration>  The expiration time on or after the generated key will no longer be accepted. This is a time span offset (e.g. "7.00:00:00" for 7 days) that will be added to the current date time to create the expiration date time. [default: 7.00:00:00]
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generate api key and hash for authentication.

Usage:
  dotnet-monitor generatekey [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <output>          The output format of the API Key configuration. [default: Json]
  -e, --expiration <expiration>  The expiration time on or after the generated key will no longer be accepted. This is a time span offset (e.g. "7.00:00:00" for 7 days) that will be added to the current date time to create the expiration date time. [default: 7.00:00:00]
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information
